task: I’m trying to sort out a phone call I have to make about a bill that doesn’t look right, and I want to be sure I don’t miss anything important. Could you help me plan what to ask and what details I should write down while I’m on the call?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple call checklist you can keep beside you so you don’t miss anything.

## Phone call checklist for a bill issue

### 1) Before you call: gather these first
- The bill or statement
- Any account number
- The date on the bill
- Any prior letters, emails, or notices
- A pen and paper, or a notes app

### 2) What to ask first
- “Can you tell me exactly what this charge is for?”
- “What dates or services does this bill cover?”
- “Why does this amount look different from what I expected?”
- “Can you check whether this bill has already been paid, adjusted, or corrected?”
- “Is there someone else I should speak to if this is not the right department?”

### 3) Details to write down during the call
Write these down as you go:
- Date and time of the call
- Name of the person you spoke with
- Their department
- Their direct phone number or extension
- Any reference or case number
- What they said the bill is for
- Any promise they made
- Any deadline they gave you
- What happens next

### 4) Important follow-up questions
- “Can you send me something in writing?”
- “What should I do next?”
- “Do I need to send any documents?”
- “If I disagree, how do I file a dispute?”
- “When should I call back if this is not fixed?”

### 5) Words that mean you should slow down and double-check
If you hear these, write them down exactly:
- “final notice”
- “past due”
- “respond by”
- “within 10 days”
- “collection”
- “dispute”
- “adjustment”
- “do not send originals”

### 6) After the call
- Compare what they said to the bill
- Check whether you need to send anything
- Put the deadline on your calendar
- Keep your notes with the bill

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page call script** you can read from during the phone call.

user_simulator.text:
Yes, that’s the kind of thing I needed. Clear and plain.

I’d probably add one more thing to ask: whether the bill was sent to the right address or account, because sometimes that’s where the trouble starts. And I’d want to write down the exact amount they say is due, not just the balance, in case it changes later.

The checklist is good. I can use that.
